programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: Extinct or Alive

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A game of underwater copycat shows how smart dolphins really are. Plus, how hyenas can eat pretty much anything and geckos' ability to apparently defy gravity

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Dean, who thought Brenda and her cat Kashmir would be the perfect fit to his home. Plus, two felines turn their new space into a giant litter box

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Ingrid, Anicka and Nikki, whose long-standing friendship is being threatened by the appalling behaviour of Ingrid's cat, Milo

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.desc: Gelison arrives at the Sepilok sanctuary aged just one year old and was traumatised after losing his mother. Although he is settling in better, his bullying tactics push limits

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: It is feeding time at the sanctuary, and the primate inhabitants must learn the tricky task of finding food for themselves

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How is one fish blowing scientists away with its brainpower? How have dogs learnt to melt our hearts? Do chimps laugh at the same jokes we do?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: The team is in Modimolle, South Africa, to search for the mighty whip scorpion. This shape-shifting arachnid is one of the world's most gifted nocturnal predators

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: The guys are in Manambato, Madagascar, to track down the tomato frog. One of the world's most remarkable ambush predators, it can dig into the ground in seconds

programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Great White Shark Babies
programme.desc: Dr Mauricio Hoyos teams up with Michelle Jewell and Toby Daly-Engel to search for the location where the pregnant great white sharks of Guadalupe give birth to their pups

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.sub-title: Lady in White/Thump in the Night
programme.desc: A couple's rabbits begin behaving nervously, then they witness the appearance of a strange figure and their pets start dying

programme.title: Monster Tag
programme.desc: Top athletes including Olympic champion, Lindsey Vonn, team up with scientists to learn about the ocean's top predators, using the latest satellite tagging technology

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How is one fish blowing scientists away with its brainpower? How have dogs learnt to melt our hearts? Do chimps laugh at the same jokes we do?

programme.title: The Good Old Days

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 22, 1999. Featuring Macy Gray, B*Witched, ATB, Honeyz, Liam Gallagher and Steve Craddock, Steps, Tina Turner and Christina Aguilera.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 29, 1999. Featuring Tina Turner, Simply Red, Eternal, Will Smith, A.T.F.C. Presents OnePhatDeeva, Destiny's Child and Westlife

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Gary Davies present the May 16 edition, featuring Kim Wilde, Bryan Ferry, Duran Duran, Jimmy Nail, Debarge, Phyllis Nelson, Paul Hardcastle and Divine

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: David Jensen and John Peel present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 10, 1984. Includes appearances from Belle & the Devotions, Queen, the Flying Pickets, Bob Marley & the Wailers, Terri Wells, the Pointer Sisters and Duran Duran

programme.title: Wild Scotland
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan explores his native Mull and some of the nearby islands, filming otters, deer, puffins, seals and a minke whale

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A January 1975 edition of the old-time music hall show from the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds, in which chairman Leonard Sachs welcomes performers including Ken Dodd, Barry Kent, Lyn Kennington, John Bouchier and Saxburger to the stage

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A friend of Hetty's asks the sleuth to represent her at a creditor's meeting and try to recover her money when a furniture shop goes bankrupt, but there more to the case than meets the eye. Back at home, Hetty's interfering brother-in-law Frank has come to stay and Robert has flown off to Australia to visit their son Derek

programme.title: Michael Palin: The Art of Travel
programme.desc: Michael discovers that the Great Western Railway had the most sophisticated public relations machine of all the railway companies between the two World Wars, producing high-quality publications and promotional gimmicks

programme.title: The Teachers' Lounge (2023)
programme.desc: When one of her students is suspected of theft, a teacher tries to get to the bottom of it. Caught between her ideals and the school system, the consequences of her actions threaten to break her. German drama, starring Leonie Benesch, Anne-Kathrin Gummich, Rafael Stachowiak and Michael Klammer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth dreads the idea of attending Onslow's birthday celebrations. However, she changes her mind when she learns a limousine will be arriving to take her to the party. Starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy's rich aunt pays him a visit, bringing out his nagging mother's snobbish streak, and he also has an encounter with a garage owner and his dogs

programme.title: Ian Hislop Goes Off the Rails
programme.desc: The story behind the 1963 Beeching Report, commissioned under Harold Macmillan's government, which led to the closure of a third of Britain's rail network. With contributions from experts, campaigners, railwaymen and passengers, Private Eye editor Ian Hislop outlines the historical background to the proposals, along with the social and economic impact that followed their implementation

programme.title: Living Famously
programme.desc: A profile of the ballet dancer. whose electrifying performances and enigmatic personality made him ballet's first superstar. First broadcast in 2003

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A friend of Hetty's asks the sleuth to represent her at a creditor's meeting and try to recover her money when a furniture shop goes bankrupt, but there more to the case than meets the eye. Back at home, Hetty's interfering brother-in-law Frank has come to stay and Robert has flown off to Australia to visit their son Derek

programme.title: Personal Cinema: Maggie Smith
programme.desc: In a programme first broadcast in 1970, the actress talks to Michael Aspel about her career and chooses scenes from some of her favourite films, including Way Out West, The Last Hurrah, On the Waterfront, Some Like It Hot and The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie

programme.title: Ian Hislop Goes Off the Rails
programme.desc: The story behind the 1963 Beeching Report, commissioned under Harold Macmillan's government, which led to the closure of a third of Britain's rail network. With contributions from experts, campaigners, railwaymen and passengers, Private Eye editor Ian Hislop outlines the historical background to the proposals, along with the social and economic impact that followed their implementation

programme.title: The Classical Collection
programme.desc: The natural world has always been a powerful inspiration to composers. From vast forests and tiny fish to wild storms and epic seascapes, this programme takes viewers on an evocative journey through some of the best-loved musical responses to the living planet. Including works by Vivaldi, Reiner, Vaughan Williams and Debussy

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Romeo and Juliet (1966)
programme.desc: Filmed performance of Prokofiev's ballet based on Shakespeare's classic love story, starring Rudolf Nureyev and Margot Fonteyn as the star-cross'd lovers forced apart by their familes' bitter rivalry. Choreographed by Kenneth McMillan, and also starring David Blair and Desmond Doyle

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn explores the work of some of the greatest dance pioneers, from the Commedia dell'Arte in 17th-century Italy to Martha Graham in 20th-century America, with performances by Margot Fonteyn and Mikhail Baryshnikov of Fokine's famous ballet, Le Spectre de la Rose

programme.title: Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: A tribute to the ballerina, first broadcast shortly after her death in 1991, with interviews with colleagues and friends and extracts from ballets from throughout her career

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The world wars force artists to grapple with destruction and savagery, challenging conventions and the old imperial order, while those fleeing war bring new ideas to Britain. Contemporary war photographer Oliver Chanarin traces the story of William Orpen's subversive protest image, To the Unknown British Soldier in France, picturing a lone draped coffin amid the magnificence of the Palace of Versailles where peace delegates met in 1919, while artist Ryan Gander examines how sculptor Barbara Hepworth tried to make sense of war by reaching for beauty in abstract human forms and Denzil Forrester looks ahead to the post-colonial aftermath of war

programme.title: The Classical Collection
programme.desc: A celebration of the newly recognised glories of classical music, featuring a wealth of works written by largely forgotten or neglected composers, including Samuel Coleridge-Taylor, Florence B Price, Grace Williams and Erich Korngold

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Chef Chris Roberts explores Anglesey, learning how a centuries-old salt-making tradition has evolved into a world-class craft, meeting a fisherman keeping the art of catching native Welsh prawns alive and visiting local beekeeper Katie, prompting him to prepare a honey cake that celebrates the sweetness and craftsmanship the bees bring to this corner of the island

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the 145th staging of the final, held at Wembley Stadium. Despite experiencing a tumultuous campaign which has seen manager Enzo Maresca and his successor Liam Rosenior both leave the club, Chelsea reached this showpiece match when they defeated Leeds United 1-0 in the last four. City are looking to win a domestic treble, having already won the League Cup, but they lost to Palace 1-0 in last term's final and briefly looked to be heading out of the competition in the semi-finals this time around, before late goals by Nico Gonzalez and Jeremy Doku gave them a 2-1 win over Southampton. With analysis from MIcah Richards and Wayne Rooney, reports from Kelly Somers, and commentary by Guy Mowbray and Alan Shearer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the revival of the comedy quiz show, in which Kym Marsh, Jenny Ryan, Babatunde Aleshe, Chris McCausland, Michael Dapaah and Chizzy Akudolu fill in the blanks to help contestants win some brilliant prizes

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan asks general knowledge questions against the clock as contestants Jill Scott, Laura Tobin, Nella Rose, Simon Rimmer, Jaz Singh, Stephen Bailey, Joel Corry and Kate Quilton try to avoid being voted off the team to win a potential prize pot of £50,000 for their charity

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Victoria Swarovski and Michael Ostrowski present the 70th song contest from the Wiener Stadthalle arena in Vienna, where the 20 acts that made it through the semi-finals, plus the host nation and the pre-qualified 'big four' - Italy, Germany, France and the UK - will be taking the stage in the hope of winning over the judges and the voters at home. Graham Norton will be providing his usual commentary, and representing the UK is electro act Look Mum No Computer, hoping to avoid nul points with his song Eins, Zwei, Drei

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Keen but accident-prone dragon Zog gets himself into mischief while learning how to fly, roar, breathe fire and fight knights in his first four years at Dragon School. Each year he meets a kindly young girl who patches up his bumps and bruises, but can she help him with his trickiest challenge yet? It's year five and he has to capture a princess. Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's book, featuring the voices of Lenny Henry, Tracey Ullman, Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Kit Harington

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Animated series based on Julia Donaldson's book about an accident-prone dragon. Zog and the doctors find a friend for Fergus

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Zog must learn to love flying again after a bad crash

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Gary Pe guide the teams in Ardingly, West Sussex, while presenter Eric Knowles learns how lightweight baskets called trugs are made

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Documentary showcasing the wonders, secrets and fragilities of the American continents, beginning with the creatures that live along North America's Atlantic coast, from the wild horses of Chesapeake Bay and the young raccoon learning the challenges of urban survival in New York City to the young black bears foraging in the Appalachian forests. Up in the air, bald eagles chase ospreys for a hard-won meal, while beneath the waters sand tiger sharks act as unlikely guardians for schools of scad fish. Narrated by Tom Hanks

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: A look at Mexico's extraordinary animal behaviours and environments. Featuring pygmy owls guiding chicks from their nest during daylight hours, orcas using complex team strategies to hunt dolphins, and orchid bees gathering up to 40 different scent. Cameras also venture into caves that are less explored than the moon

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: Retired ferryman John Murray shows his grandson Tom some old home-movie footage of the opening of the Strangford car ferry in 1969

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: A round-up of the results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Emma suspects the pond is being fished by more than their local fishermen and trail cameras capture the visit of a family of hungry otters. The Williamsons take their ram lambs to a prestigious sale on the mainland hoping to make big money and secure their reputation as top tup breeders, while Callum and Zara's children Lucy and Lottie enter the young handlers competition at the Arran agricultural show

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley explore the River Leven in Fife, revealing how this industrial waterway shaped communities for centuries and how it is being transformed. Anita joins archaeologists on a major new excavation digging into the river's hidden past, before meeting the people helping reconnect local communities with the water for the first time in generations. At Loch Leven, Joe gets hands-on with one of the river's last working sluices to discover how its waters powered industry, and why it still plays an important role today. Meanwhile, on his farm in the Cotswolds, Adam Henson's annual spring plant surveys reveal how the landscape is changing, year by year

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Belfast's Botanic Gardens, where treasures include a teddy bear made to mark the sinking of the Titanic, an early work by artist Norah McGuinness, and the dress medals of one of the founding soldiers of the SAS. Charlotte McReynolds, curator from National Museums NI, shows Fiona the hand-embroidered floral Lennox quilt, made in the early 1700s, and Katherine Wright shares the story behind some fine pieces of Belfast glassware

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Comic crime drama about a detective who teams up with the retired actor who played her cop show hero. A community service group comes under suspicion when one of their members is pushed off the tower of a ruined castle around which the group are working. But John's relationship with Janie's mother disrupts their ability to crack the case. Timothy Spall and Gwyneth Keyworth are joined by Jane Horrocks and Alexandra Roach

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty and Leanne are pulled in different directions. At a tense casino night, Gary's money laundering unravels

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park. The match between the Villans and Liverpool was likely to have a major bearing on which side finished highest in the table, while the Hammers continued their bid to avoid relegation away to midtable Newcastle. Plus, Manchester United v Nottingham Forest, Brentford v Crystal Palace, Everton v Sunderland, Leeds United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Fulham

programme.title: Wayne Rooney Meets
programme.desc: The former England striker talks to Everton and Three Lions goalkeeper Jordan Pickford

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The ultimate hunt for the best British comic begins as 30 hopefuls battle it out across six heats to earn a place in the final, where one will be crowned the BBC New Comedian of 2025. Josh Jones hosts the West heat at St George's, a fully accessible live music and arts venue in the heart of Bristol. Carwyn Blayney, Will Owen, Omari Douglas, Jess Carrivick and Alfie Dundas take to the stage in a bid to impress judges Priya Hall, Harriet Dyer and Fatiha El-Ghorri

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Very average woman Spud cooks, cleans, serves and cares for her father. The only problem is she does it all rather badly - and half-heartedly. A woman who is never seen, unattractive and ignored, she does not feel bad about herself. However, when her father dies, she starts to realise she may have to come out from the shadow and be seen. Comedy drama, written by and starring Siobhán McSweeney

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the final round of fixtures in the Super League, including Liverpool v Arsenal, West Ham United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Manchester United

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor becomes convinced that a mysterious creature always just out of sight is the root cause of everyone's childhood fears, and resolves to track it down. Dragging Clara away from a disastrous date with new work colleague Danny Pink, he goes in search of answers. Their pursuit of the creature through time takes them to the end of the universe, but also to Danny's childhood

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison realises that Kitty's fond memories of her sister conceal a darker truth, and tries to help her come to terms with her past. The rest of the ghosts fear that getting Kitty to open up will just be traumatic for everyone - but end up sharing some unresolved issues of their own. Mike resorts to witchcraft in a bid to see the ghosts for himself

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison spends more time with Lucy and starts planning to make a big investment - but the Captain and Kitty uncover something suspicious, and need Fanny's newfound sleuthing skills to get to the bottom of the mystery before it is too late. Thomas works on his football skills with a little help form Humphrey's head, and Julian lends an unseen helping hand to Mike's promo film

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A spate of local burglaries prompts Agnes to increase her home security, fitting a bolt to the back door and a state-of-the-art alarm system provided by Buster Brady. If only he didn't get all his gear off the back of a van. The bank manager refuses Dermot a business loan so his mum becomes determined to make the money for him - by winning the local poker tournament, which means going up against arch-rival and reigning champion Hillary. Cathy is feeling broody and decides to pursue unconventional means to get pregnant. Agnes is left reeling at her announcement - but a lovelorn Buster sees it as an opportunity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Alex Murphy. Conor and Jock finally reunite after Jock makes a lucky escape from the Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The lads decide it's time to expand their empire in Cork city, as their days of doing all the hard work are over - they plan on being the bosses now. Starring Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor's one true love Linda Walsh is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: The model shares the story of her 2017 abduction and tries to come to terms with what took place, the media reaction and why many people refused to believe her. Alongside Chloe's unfolding account of what happened, the series features archive footage and testimony from her former agent, detectives from the Italian and British police, the Italian legal team and the judge in the kidnappers' trial

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet struggles to make the right decision when Gaz confesses his love to her. On discovering Gaz's relationship with Janet, Donna vandalises his van - but has an affair of her own to hide. Meanwhile, Munch tries to find a suitable birthing expert to help Louise

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet twists Gaz's arm into having a romantic night in with her after he suggests going out and letting his former girlfriend babysit Corinthian. However, the mood turns sour when the couple begin to disagree over Jonny, prompting Gaz to make a big commitment

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise struggles with her duties as a mum - leaving her baby behind whenever she goes out - and receives much-needed advice from Tim. Meanwhile, Janet is unsure about Donna's new boyfriend. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith and Natalie Casey

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet responds to being mugged by becoming a police community support officer. Wesley returns to the area intent on seeking advice from Gaz on the best way to win Donna's heart, while Louise runs into problems as she tries to find a father figure for her daughter. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith, Will Mellor, Thomas Nelstrop and Kathryn Drysdale

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The ultimate hunt for the best British comic begins as 30 hopefuls battle it out across six heats to earn a place in the final, where one will be crowned the BBC New Comedian of 2025. Josh Jones hosts the West heat at St George's, a fully accessible live music and arts venue in the heart of Bristol. Carwyn Blayney, Will Owen, Omari Douglas, Jess Carrivick and Alfie Dundas take to the stage in a bid to impress judges Priya Hall, Harriet Dyer and Fatiha El-Ghorri

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Nationwide search to find the best new stand-up talent in the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian makes a worrying discovery, Nicola takes matters into her own hands, and Grant helps a friend

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea finds herself in hot water, Mark attempts to ask for help, while George and Nicola face questions from their family

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant makes a situation worse, Bea panics about her actions, and Nicola realises she is out of options

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea is out of options, Mark and Grant's relationship is more strained than ever, while Cindy tries to get through to George

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: God's Creatures (2022)
programme.desc: Aileen's willingness to help her recently returned son, Brian, has major repercussions in their struggling County Donegal fishing community. Drama, starring Emily Watson, Paul Mescal and Aisling Franciosi

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: The case of a Somerset high-flyer who turned his charm and connections into tools of deception, conning his friends and hundreds of others, while living a life of glamour himself. Through the voices of his victims and event industry insiders, this documentary uncovers the high personal and financial cost of chasing exclusivity, and how easily even the most cautious can be deceived

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Chloe reveals a turning point in her captivity, when she realised she might be able to persuade one of the gang to help her, and recalls leaving the farmhouse with her captor, uncertain if she was being handed over to the authorities or sold into sex slavery

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: The glamour model and kidnapping victim recalls her ordeal, orchestrated by Black Death. Once safely under the protection of the Italian authorities, she had to give evidence in court against the man who enabled her escape from the international sex-trafficking gang - Lukasz Herba. Chloe then returned home to the UK, to find herself plunged into ordeal as the Italian courts publicly released details of the case and the ongoing investigation to the world's press. What she hoped to bury as an embarrassing nightmare soon ignited the interest of the global media

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Sarah Keyworth hosts, as second heat contenders Eli Hart, Farhan Solo, Chloe Reynolds, Will BF and Monica Hsueh take to the stage of Birmingham's Hockley Social Club in a bid to book their place in the final. Head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri is joined in her ranks by comedian, writer and podcaster Aurie Styla and local Brum stand-up Lindsey Santoro

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor becomes convinced that a mysterious creature always just out of sight is the root cause of everyone's childhood fears, and resolves to track it down. Dragging Clara away from a disastrous date with new work colleague Danny Pink, he goes in search of answers. Their pursuit of the creature through time takes them to the end of the universe, but also to Danny's childhood

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Hard-hitting drama written by and starring Richard Gadd - last seen in Netflix thriller Baby Reindeer - and charting the explosive friendship between two men over a 30-year period, since they were brought into each other's lives as youngsters through death and circumstance. In 1993, Niall is conflicted when asked by his mother to lie in court about Ruben's violence. He seems to have changed for the better, so the decision weighs heavily on him. Co-starring Jamie Bell, Marianne McIvor and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Mastermind: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: The publication of Ann's groundbreaking study, which was followed by a decline in serial crimes. However, she quickly comes to realize her work was not done

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Grace Dent present the third quarter-final. The hopefuls begin with an invention test in which they must come up with a brunch dish of their own choosing, making the most of the fresh produce on offer. Then Jimi Famurewa arrives to set them a brief, asking for a dish that uses a nut or nuts of their choice

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay puts the spotlight onto his hometown of Bolton, as TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz face a fresh test of knowledge. Also, Paul Gorton delivers the tour, complete with a few extra treats along the way

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Exhausted with requests for help, Looph deliberately disappears, forcing the Piris to turn detective, searching the berg until her ingenious plan is finally revealed

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima accuses Big Lizard of eating Dad's birthday cake

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings try to get rid of a bat that has interrupted their game, but Weasy thinks it's really cool and ditches them to become a comic-book hero with a bat sidekick

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: To avoid Herbert's surprise visits via his tunnels in the Big Birch, the other heroes take any quest and must deal with an angry plant

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a school of crabs that live in an underwater city.

programme.title: Duck & Frog
programme.desc: The duo have a job tidying the Mayor's garden, but then Frog builds an automatic lawnmower that soon causes chaos, leaving Duck to get everything back in order

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Liko and Meowscarada face off against a fellow Nidothing fan who really loves Litten

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum has taken Adam and Joe's phones, and they must abseil down the world's tallest abseil tower to get them back. Joel M teaches Joe a trick or two about disappearing

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.sub-title: Apollo, Hammer, Fire, Nitro v the Exercise Class!
programme.desc: Daring each other with silly in-ear prompts, Apollo, Hammer, Nitro and Fire go head to head at a fitness studio

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The gang go rallying and look ahead to Eurovision

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Comedian Bill Bailey narrates a documentary examining the lives of an eccentric cast of unconventional and unusual creatures that do things differently, turning this oddness to their advantage, such as the parrot that has forgotten how to fly, the bear that has turned vegetarian and a penguin that lives in the forest

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes a start on planting up some of his spectacular summer containers. He's also got leeks to plant out and shares some advice on how to make your own fertiliser. Frances Tophill heads to Berkshire to learn more about biodynamic gardening and the benefits it can bring. It's also time for an update from Adam Frost's new garden, where things are slowly starting to take shape

programme.title: How to Marry a Millionaire (1953)
programme.desc: Three women rent a luxurious New York apartment as part of a scheme to get their claws into wealthy husbands - but their plans become unstuck when all three start to fall for men with good hearts and empty pockets. Comedy, starring Betty Grable, Marilyn Monroe, Lauren Bacall, William Powell, Cameron Mitchell, David Wayne, Rory Calhoun and Fred Clark

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening meeting of the campaign from China Textile City Sports Center in Shaoxing, China. Six meeting records fell here last year, including a clearance of 6.11m by dominant Swedish pole-vaulter Armand Duplantis and an American sprint hurdles double courtesy of Cordell Tinch and Grace Stark

programme.title: Andi Oliver's Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi visits the seaside and harbour town of Bridlington in East Yorkshire, where she plans to throw a party to secure the future of its historic fishing industry. Andi takes a group of local teenagers on a fishing trip to convince them to sign up for a career on the high seas, and comes up with a shellfish feast that appeals to all, using regional produce

programme.title: Great Bear Stakeout
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary following the grizzly bears of the Alaskan wilderness as they come out of hibernation and are thrust together in a frenzy of feeding, fighting and mating. The opening programme introduces first-time grizzly mum Parsnip and her young cub Pushki. Only half of bear cubs make it through the season and they are both struggling to survive in the unforgiving region, which is ruled by massive male Van and his aggressive partner Alice - for whom Parsnip's youngster would make a good meal. Narrated by Billy Connolly

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young canine-loving couple from Hertfordshire are hoping for a fresh start in a new High Weald home. Their budget is £600,000, so presenter Ginny Buckley soon rustles up some tempting properties to show them. Away from the houses, Ginny picks some of the local grape harvest before treating herself to a glass of fizz

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire as hundreds of visitors arrive with their family heirlooms and the experts pick out the best items to go off to auction. Paul also investigates the history of nearby Quarrybank Mill

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Acting friends Peter Gunn and Melanie Hill join experts James Braxton and David Harper on a search through Staffordshire and Derbyshire to find antiques to sell at auction

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: A look at some of the UK's most beautiful and historic homes, and people who are saving the past for all of us. From a fragile silk robe and a wartime wall painting to a writer's handmade timepiece, the hidden treasures in two houses reveal the stories of two of Britain's most celebrated authors - and the expert teams racing to preserve them. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: Darkest Hour (2017)
programme.desc: May 1940. As the Nazis advance against Britain's European allies, newly elected prime minister Winston Churchill must decide whether to leave neighbouring countries to their fate, by striking a peace deal with Hitler, or fight on against terrifying odds for the greater good. Double Oscar and Bafta-winning period drama, starring Gary Oldman, Lily James and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Their Finest (2016)
programme.desc: Former secretary Catrin Cole, newly appointed by the British Ministry of Information as a scriptwriter for propaganda films, joins the cast and crew of a major production while the Blitz rages around them. Comedy drama, starring Gemma Arterton, Sam Claflin, Richard E Grant and Bill Nighy

programme.title: LGBTQ+ Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances by stars whose sex and sexuality is central to their careers and the songs they've created, including George Michael, Boy George, Freddie Mercury, Beth Ditto, MNEK, Lil Nas X and Barry Manilow. Some pushed boundaries and challenged gender stereotypes from the very start, while others only revealed their true selves after years of success and speculation, but all have played their part in changing the world of popular music

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon and Dylan finally spend time together. He wants to mend their relationship, but she is not ready to listen, until a journey into his past helps her understand. Meanwhile, a different side of Ollie comes to light as she encounters an old foe. Cat's insecurities get the better of her, and she doesn't know where to turn, until she and Ollie finally find common ground

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon is ready to take control of her life, even if it means building dangerous new alliances. Determined to protect her family, she turns her attention to Sam. As chaos builds, she, Cat and Ollie become increasingly desperate, until Shannon makes a bold decision

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter C, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region's largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: A special edition from the Isle of Bute, where Dougie Vipond meets dairy farmersand also visits the grounds of Mount Stuart House to delve into one of the island's biggest wildlife mysteries. Arlene Stuart joins a beach ranger at Scalpsie Bay to find out what is washing up on Bute's shores, while Shahbaz Majeed meets a Syrian family who fled war and have built a new life on the island

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter visits Glenarn, which has a fabulous collection of rhododendrons looking their best. Calum Clunie demonstrates how to take cuttings from a fuchsia, and there are ideas on how to manage a plot if you are unlucky enough to have club root in your vegetable patch

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Rukmini Iyer, Stephen Terry, Ken Hom and Anna Haugh, while singer-songwriter James Bay faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Live Women's Six Nations
programme.desc: Wales v Italy (Kick-off 12.15pm). Coverage of both teams' fifth and final match of the championship, held at Cardiff Arms Park. Italy claimed a comfortable 44-12 victory when the teams met at Stadio Sergio Lanfranchi last season, but Wales claimed a 22-20 victory at Principality Stadium in 2024 and will hope to make home advantage count once again

programme.title: Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953)
programme.desc: Lounge singers Lorelei and Dorothy embark on a transatlantic cruise to Paris in search of rich husbands. While Dorothy falls for a penniless man, Lorelei thinks she has found the millionaire of her dreams, only for his father to hire a private eye to spy on her and prove she is a gold-digger. Howard Hawks' musical comedy, starring Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Charles Coburn and Elliott Reid

programme.title: The Championship
programme.desc: Armagh v Monaghan (Throw-in 4.15pm). Sarah Mulkerrins presents coverage of the showpiece match, held at St Tiernach's Park. Armagh comfortably defeated Down in the last four to give them a chance of a first triumph in this competition since 2008, while Monaghan overcame Derry in the semi-finals and last lifted this trophy in 2015. With analysis by Oisín McConville and Philly McMahon, pitchside updates from Mark Sidebottom, and commentary by Thomas Niblock, joined by Conor McManus

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: Snapshots of summer in the less urban parts of the county, where people's lives are rooted in nature and the seasons that shape it

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents a collection of the best finds from the show's travels round the country

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist and explorer ventures into more uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries and world firsts, beginning by heading to the Kamchatka Peninsula in the far east of Russia. He joins a team of world-class kayakers to take on the first descent of the Kronotsky River, setting out to paddle from source to sea, starting in the shadow of a still-active volcano and ending at the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.sub-title: Countdown to RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost take a first look at the event, with Rachel de Thame and JJ Chalmers joining to explore the latest garden design ideas across the showground and the impressive variety of floral exhibits in the Great Pavilion. Plus, there's the reveal of the RHS and the King's Foundation Curious Garden designed by Frances Tophill, and championed by King Charles, David Beckham and Alan Titchmarsh. Gardeners' World presenter Arit Anderson unveils her first ever judged garden at Chelsea in aid of Parkinson's UK

programme.title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The second leg of Simon's journey takes him from the fjords of Norway across the North Atlantic to Iceland, known as the land of fire and ice. It's a world of extremes and contradictions, from a country that's been described as the Kuwait of Europe to an island that lives under the constant threat of earthquakes and volcanic eruptions

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools returns to Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London for the 68th run of the show. Former One Direction member Niall Horan performs a couple of songs, including the title track from his forthcoming album Dinner Party, while veteran pop band Squeeze also play and chat to Jools at the piano about how their new record Trixies came about. Also appearing are dance-pop duo Tomora, who are Norwegian singer-songwriter Aurora and Tom Rowlands of the Chemical Brothers, as well as American poet and activist Aja Monet and Bristol rock and pop duo Getdown Services

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Brother (2022)
programme.desc: Sons of Caribbean immigrants, Francis and Michael face questions of masculinity, identity and family amid the pulsing beat of Toronto's early hip-hop scene. . Coming-of-age drama, starring Lamar Johnson and Aaron Pierre

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonated inside Harvey's Wagon Wheel Casino, it left behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel in the form of a maze of unanswered questions. However, a lucky break in the case led to a psychological stand-off where calculated risks unravelled, loyalties fractured, and the truth proved far more complicated than anyone imagined

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Susan Calman, Rosie Jones and Josh Widdicombe join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz, with host Sandi Toksvig asking questions on a vintage theme

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: The stories of some of the two million British children who were not evacuated during the Second World War, staying at home in towns and cities at risk of being bombed, often to help their families or because their parents couldn't bear to send them away. This documentary features some of the last people who were witness to the reality of the Blitz, with their testimonies offering an intimate, enlightening and moving portrait of wartime Britain through children's eyes

programme.desc: Andy McGrath embarks on an inspiring journey back to Scotland to explore Rosslyn Chapel and its intriguing connection to the Holy Grail, made famous by the Da Vinci Code books

programme.desc: A strange structure spotted in Mongolia may be the lost tomb of Genghis Khan, and bizarre rings in Spain could lead to the lost city of Atlantis

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A bright pink lake could point to a strange, undiscovered new species. The legends of ancient giants surrounding a mysterious monument in the Holy Land. Plus, how satellite images help with the investigation into a missing boy

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: Lacking in speed and might, the SS Andrea Doria more than made up for it in beauty, but on the night of 25 July 1956 - the Italian liner met a disastrous fate

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: SS America was an ocean liner and cruise ship built in the US in 1940 for the United States Lines, designed by the noted naval architect William Francis Gibbs. She served her owners for 55 years until she was unceremoniously left abandoned in the Canary Islands

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The actor explores the thrilling world of vintage daredevils, from the 'human fly' to skateboarding speedsters and diving horses. These bold risk-takers chased adrenaline with unforgettable, dangerous stunts

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: For centuries, powerful artifacts have sparked wars, inspired legends and shaped empires. From divine weapons, to formidable places, to sacred relics, these are the incredible stories of the world's most mighty marvels

programme.title: Battle Treasures with Foxy and Bruce
programme.desc: Jason Fox and Bruce Crompton explore artefacts of courage and survival that defined the culture of air combat, from Martin-Baker Vermont watches to bomber jackets

programme.title: Battle Treasures with Foxy and Bruce
programme.desc: The SAS respirator, KGB umbrella, and Saddam's gold pistol reveal the daring, secrecy and ruthlessness of special operations from London to Baghdad. Presented by Jason Fox and Bruce Crompton

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: In Worcestershire, Andy McGrath embarks on an enchanting journey to a haunted bookstore once graced by Charles Dickens, investigates the captivating mystery of King John's missing bones, and stands in awe beneath an ancient oak that sheltered William Shakespeare

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The mysteries of a vanishing lake, toxic hell storms, CIA-trained killer monks and polar bears that stalked stricken Second World War heroes

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A stroll through Great Sand Dunes National Park and Preserve in southern Colorado, where strange lights, animal mutilations and other bizarre incidents have been spotted

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Reports of odd sightings at Lassen Volcanic National Park, in California, which include reptiles - known as reptoids - who work with aliens and can appear in human form

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Farmer Edwin Fuhr recalls harvesting crops when he saw a metal object hovering in his field. Almost immediately, four identical objects appeared

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: The story of strange lights witnessed over a ranch in Argentina. Plus, Henry Baker who alongside his wife spottrf lights hovering over Lake Erie

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: A dog-walker who spotted a large cylindrical object hovering in the sky, and schoolchildren in Florida who claim to have witnessed multiple UFOs

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Two patrol cops in Ohio spot a strange bright light flying through the night sky, and are given orders to pursue the object, triggering a large-scale car chase

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: A wave of UFO sightings in the Brazilian city of Colares causes mass panic, and in Australia, a flying object causes a serious problems for a farmer

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Officials struggle to decide how to respond when a UFO is spotted hovering over an air force base, and a priest claims to have interacted with a saucer-shaped metallic craft

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Two brothers out hunting in Idaho witness something that defies explanation, and a strange metallic craft passes over Antananarivo on the island of Madagascar

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Texas police receive several calls about an unusual flying object, and a property inspector is knocked unconscious when he comes in contact with a strange metallic object

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les shocks the others when he reveals his plans to buy a new store. Meanwhile, a man wanting to pawn his VCR causes a scene, and another tries to get money for his fake eye

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les goes into detail about his plans for a new pawn store, and Ashley is caught in the crossfire when a fight breaks out in the middle of the floor

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: At the new shop, the Golds are surprised to find a bag of gems inside a former employee's desk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Golds have a decision to make when a man wants to buy a pair of very expensive earrings on the condition that the store also does his piercing

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Former employee Tressa returns to sell the Rolex that got her sacked and Les struggles to control the situation when insults start to fly between her and Ashley

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les goes on the rampage when he finds out a thief has been on the premises, and a customer selling a guitar signed by Kid Rock reveals a valuable secret

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley comes out from behind the security glass to confront a menacing customer, and there is more trouble when a man claims he has been robbed in the shop

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley is in trouble with Seth and Les, who discover she is to blame for the shop's current cash crisis

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Pawn Stars Do America
programme.desc: Rick Harrison, Corey Harrison and Austin 'Chumlee' Russell head to the nation's capital, Washington DC, where they call on some trusted experts to help lay down the law for good deals. In between the event, Chum and Corey get carried away in a massive mansion

programme.title: Pawn Stars Do America

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: The biggest auction on the planet sails to the middle of the Pacific Ocean when a mysterious container is dredged up from the bottom of the sea

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: The fast-paced world of shipping port auctions, where bidders need nerves of steel to beat their rivals

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Mo and the Israelis team up to buy a container from Africa, and things take a bizarre turn when a bunch of strange items from an odd collector sparks an intense bidding war

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Four containers from New York make their way to Long Beach, California. Bidders are interested in a billionaire's unclaimed goods and a one-of-a-kind auction is held

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: A can of outdoor adventure equipment holds a surprise and a tailgater's dream could stop its winner short of the goal line

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: The fast-paced world of shipping port auctions. The Israelis go after what they think is vintage clothing. Meanwhile, a custom container could land a buyer in jail

programme.sub-title: Does a Bear Bid in the Woods?
programme.desc: Kenny makes his way out to the mountains, constantly watching out for cougars. Lisa scores on a small sister locker, but it's Brandi who gets the nomination for buyer of the week

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy kidnaps Pops and takes him to Rosamond, where they deftly score a fine locker. Kenny sets out to brighten everyone's day and his clowning around has him seeing stars. Lisa proves she has a friend - for now

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: If Ya Got It, Flaut It
programme.desc: Kenny happens upon a familiar vehicle and follows it all the way to Lancaster for an auction - where he tries desperately to find his gas money

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers return to Montebello, California, where Rene and Casey unsuccessfully try to throw off the competition. Ivy secures his skating equipment

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Rene and Casey attempt to shut out Jarrod and Brandi, Darrell uncovers an interesting locker, while Ivy meets a furry friend. Reality series following people who make a living from bidding for the contents of abandoned storage units

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy tries to keep his winning streak going, while Darrell and Rene play mind games with each other. Jarrod and Brandi visit a crime scene and travel back in time

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: El Dorado is a legendary ancient city, said to be overflowing with gold. For centuries, explorers put their lives on the line searching for it. The endless quest to uncover this infamous city continues to this day

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: While drilling in the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, the team makes a discovery that could literally be out of this world

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: A nostalgic journey through America's past reveals a time when any and all cures could be advertised and sold to the public indiscriminately, without any redress being offered when they failed. Henry dives into a world where animal skin could replace one's own and where doctors gave coffee enemas

programme.title: The Witches of Salem
programme.desc: The searing, real-life horror story of the mass witch-hunt that enveloped Salem and New England in 1692. The hysteria and panic that overtook the Puritan community of Salem led to the execution of 20 innocent souls. This instlament explains how the trials were suddenly halted when the Massachusetts elite were accused of witchcraft, and Salem had to deal with the aftermath. Narrated by Mark Strong

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary revealing mysterious curiosities that were once hidden from human eyes, only to be exposed by a calamitous incident. This episode focuses on the Mary Celestia wreck, discovers imperial relics from Iraq and examines a medieval man's remains and lost artefacts reshaping Native American history

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A further look at some of the world's most strange unexplained phenomena. This second episode looks at the mysterious disappearance of US union official Jimmy Hoffa in July 1975. He was declared dead seven years later

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A look at some of the world's most strange unexplained phenomena. Since the 1950s, multiple air disasters have had one thing in common - flight number 191. Is it a coincidence, or something more sinister?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A bizarre trend happened in Ireland in the 1980s when thousands of people started seeing religious statues move. Some residents of a village reported seeing a statue of the Virgin Mary move. Others believed the phenomenon was just an optical illusion or a prank

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Many historical figures have resided in The White House and the US building is one of the most-famous and most-haunted residences in the world. Experts investigate claims of extraordinary paranormal activity

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Delving into the mysteries surrounding the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, investigating the theories that he remained alive, secretly, for some time afterwards

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: People reveal details from their past lives as celebrities, while experts investigate evidence that suggests death may not be the end of one's existence

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her home city of Bristol. She takes a horse-drawn carriage ride to explore Clifton's Georgian architecture, samples some of the backstreet gin that was considered a threat to civilisation, and discovers the ingenious story behind the world's first chocolate factory. She also delves into the city's links with the slave trade, uncovering horrific tales in the historical records. Plus, aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson provides a bird's-eye view of how 18th-century wealth transformed the city
