programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin and Perry try to befriend some girls at a party, while Tim Nice-But-Dim transforms himself into a rock star, and Lance sinks into depression when his imaginary girlfriend dumps him. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: Dan and Courtney's relaxing weekend away is interrupted when she goes into labour and Jonathan gets a phone call asking to drive them to hospital. Keen to make it up to Claire, Jonathan asks her to come along too - but her suitcase is already packed for her trip to Venice with Brett. Meanwhile, Spencer makes his own romantic gesture. Comedy, with Romesh Ranganathan and Jessica Knappett

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: Comedy about a self-made man struggling to keep his head above water as the 1980s turn into the 1990s. Martin Parker has the ambition of Alan Sugar and the swagger of Robert Kilroy Silk. With a chain of electrical stores bearing his name, he seems to have it all. However the truth is he swimming in debt, his complicated private life is catching up with him and he's starting to lose his power. Written by and starring Sian Gibson, with Conleth Hill, Rosie Cavaliero, George Costigan, Sheila Reid and Jason Barnett

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony decides to break up with his girlfriend and agonises over the best way to go about it. He unintentionally creates the ideal opportunity by cancelling a date to prepare a meal for Deborah. Starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted falls in love with a piano-playing guest at the holiday camp, but romance proves difficult with her overbearing mother in the way. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy becomes a yuppie, complete with red braces, Filofax and chrome briefcase. Meanwhile, Rodney begins attending a computer course at night school, where he meets Cassandra. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy acquires three tons of lead sheeting, which turns out to be a DIY nuclear fallout shelter, so he, Rodney and Grandad collect their sleeping bags and food and have a practice run to make sure they will know what to do if the Russians attack

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dodgy dealer Del gets his hands on a consignment of self-inflating rubber dolls, which turn out to be filled with explosive gas. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del gets his hands on a statue of a Hindu god - which plunges him into the middle of an age-old feud between rival families. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters embark on a venture involving the decoration of Denzil's flat. When they find his wife's beloved canary lying dead in its cage, they assume responsibility and race to replace it before the deal is ruined. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del enters one of Rodney's old paintings in a competition and is over the moon when he wins a holiday for three at a five-star hotel in Majorca. Soon after, the Trotter brothers and Cassandra arrive at Palma Airport - but Del-Boy reveals there is a slight snag. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: After lending assistance to a wealthy woman in distress, Del seizes a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to improve his social standing by launching his own chandelier-cleaning business. As part of the venture, he ropes a reluctant Rodney and Grandad into helping him. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del's beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nag's Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Admitted to hospital for tests, he fears there is something seriously wrong with him - but a bacon sandwich and an alcoholic drink smuggled in by Rodney soon put the smile back on his face. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del spots another sure-fire winner and decides to enter the highly profitable tourist trade - and with Rodney and Grandad backing him 100 per cent, he is sure his open-top bus tour initiative will be a massive success. Vintage comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nag's Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy becomes a yuppie, complete with red braces, Filofax and chrome briefcase. Meanwhile, Rodney begins attending a computer course at night school, where he meets Cassandra. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dodgy dealer Del gets his hands on a consignment of self-inflating rubber dolls, which turn out to be filled with explosive gas. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright tries to improve the sale of an overstocked commodity - Jamaican Ginger cake - with incredible efficiency, and Granville's lust for power leads to disaster

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.desc: Del gets his hands on a statue of a Hindu god - which plunges him into the middle of an age-old feud between rival families. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Toby makes an appearance on TV to discuss private practice. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: On a trip to Yorkshire, Claire forces Jonathan to lie to her parents about their break-up, a lie that goes way too far. Dan and Courtney go on respective mum's and dad's nights out. The professor calls Dan asking for an answer on the job, so she seeks advice from her fellow 'dads'. The nights are both very different, but Courtney's ends with a shock revelation as Dan makes a decision about her dream job. Comedy, guest starring Paula Wilcox, with Romesh Ranganathan, Jessica Knappett and Aisling Bea

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: It's the day of Dan's baby shower and Jonathan tries to end things with Megan before Claire finds out - only for a dead cat to foil his plan. The reality of having a baby alongside Dan's new job sends Courtney spiralling. Comedy, with Romesh Ranganathan and Jessica Knappett

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright tries to improve the sale of an overstocked commodity - Jamaican Ginger cake - with incredible efficiency, and Granville's lust for power leads to disaster

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del gets his hands on a statue of a Hindu god - which plunges him into the middle of an age-old feud between rival families. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee is unhappy with his new living arrangements after Lucy's father separates from his wife and moves into the flat, so he takes on the role of counsellor in a bid to patch up the marriage and get his room back. Comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Tim Vine. Tim arrives home after a night out clubbing and realises he has picked up the wrong coat, one which contains a pocket full of drugs. After discovering the owner is Larry 'the Butcher' Stubbs, he enlists Lee's help to return the jacket without ending up in serious danger

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary sells one of Dorothy's records when Tony identifies it as a valuable collector's item - but struggles to find a replacement when she asks where it has gone

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey coaches the Smith family through a mammoth declutter as their possessions are laid out in a warehouse as though it were an art installation they discover 207 loose nappies, 145 Punjabi suits and 24 old toothbrushes amongst other things. Things get emotional as Raaj lets go of some of her late sister's belongings whom she lost to Covid. Carpenter Rob works hard to make their three-bedroom house accommodate six people and Dilly creates a brand-new larder while reorganising the kitchen

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team head to Coolaney in Country Sligo to adapt the home of Claire Carpenter to her needs so she can return from hospital after suffering a stroke

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stevie questions a man arrested for launching an unprovoked attack on a fellow drinker in a pub and becomes emotionally involved when she discovers the suspect is deeply depressed after losing his job and learning his wife has been having an affair with her boss. Newly promoted Inspector Smith urges caution, but Stevie puts herself in danger after the downtrodden husband sets out for revenge - carrying a gun. Danielle Brent guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A child abandoned in a busy market alerts police to the fact his nanny has been abducted and badly beaten in what turns out to be a case of mistaken identity. Neil recognises the intended target as a psychiatrist he worked with on a previous case in which a mental health patient in her care was shot dead on his orders - so their reunion is less than cordial. Ruth Gemmell guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. When psychiatrist Julia Bickham's partner is injured in a hit-and-run incident and her nanny is attacked in what turns out to be a case of mistaken identity, Neil investigates the possibility somebody holds a grudge against her. Among the suspects is a man whose wife died two years previously from an overdose. On the strength of Julia's professional testimony that it was a case of suicide, the man lost his life assurance claim and subsequently custody of his daughter

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Banksy renews acquaintance with the Sissoulu family after a telephone used to tip off the police about a murder is traced back to grieving father Joseph, whose son Danny was recently killed in a similar manner. The dead teenager's friends are reluctant to say much, but suspicion falls on Mo Campbell when his prints are found in the newsagent's shop

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil and Max investigate the murder of a 17-year-old girl who had recently left foster care to live alone. Relations between the detectives are strained when Max makes a disparaging remark about kids placed in care at the children's home, and the case takes an unexpected twist when the prime suspect for the shooting turns out to be the partner of the victim's drug-using mother

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: After a raid, Paul Brewer is arrested for having indecent images of a four-year-old girl on his laptop. He claims to have lent his computer to convicted paedophile George Fielding, who Brewer met in prison. When questioned, Fielding admits to downloading the pictures, but tests prove they were taken by camera and were not from the internet. Banksy and Max are left to sort out which of the pair is covering for the other. Jeff Rawle guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jo persuades a former prostitute she once knew to help her catch a big-time drug dealer. After days of surveillance, a raid is mounted but draws a blank when the £40,000 consignment of drugs disappears along with one of the suspects. A second ex-prostitute connected to the case admits to knowing what happened, but refuses to help in the inquiries because she is terrified of the 'Mr Big' behind it all

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for those inside

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A man is left with a metal spike impaled in his face after a traumatic incident on a construction site, sparking a huge rescue operation. Medics help a man who was hit by a car

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman plunges 10ft through the floorboards of her Sheffield home in a freak accident, and the HART team launch a major rescue operation to save her

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: There's a major rescue operation on the M1 after a car towing a caravan rolls over and hits a lamp post - trapping the driver. A teacher on his mountain bike suffers a brain injury

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team helps a biker critically injured in a crash. In Brontë country, a man vanishes after going for a swim in a Pennines lake, sparking a major rescue operation

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A dangerous driver in a pick-up truck causes carnage when his vehicle rolls over and the HART team are called to free him and his passenger from the wreckage

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Join ambulances, helicopters and boats across New Zealand, transporting critical patients to safety. A teenage forestry worker receives a life-threatening injury, severe asthma endangers a mum's life and police help young divers safely to shore

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A woman gets caught on the wrong end of a fishing spear, there's a daring rescue at sea and a chopper crew welcomes a new life mid-flight

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The local council insists that Ben is disabled so he happily plays along with the mistake - until he comes to regret his actions. Comedy, guest starring Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.sub-title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Cash-strapped Alison and Mike think all their problems have been solved when they inherit a grand country house, unaware that it is falling apart and teeming with the ghosts of former inhabitants. The spooks must act immediately to halt the new owners' plans to turn the house into a hotel, but their attempts to scare them away fail miserably. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, Mathew Baynton, Simon Farnaby, Martha Howe-Douglas, Jim Howick, Laurence Rickard and Ben Willbond

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison finds herself hounded by the ghosts, who now realise she can both see and hear them, and tries to ignore their existence - a tactic which only exacerbates the problem when the Captain puts the ghosts onto a war footing. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the house into a home leads to a DIY disaster, saddling Lady Button with an ill-suited roommate, and Julian learns about his questionable legacy when the spooks discover the internet. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Nan Taylor, Bernie the incompetent nurse, Margaret the jumpy woman and Essex couple Paul and Sam

programme.title: Death at the Mansion: Rebecca Zahau
programme.desc: The experts conclude their theories on Rebecca's death. A civil trial verdict prompts the San Diego Sheriff's Department to make a long-awaited announcement

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy sketch series featuring the versatile actress. Bernie the Irish nurse is feeling a little romantic

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team help the Clark family declutter as they lay out their belongings in a warehouse only to discover they own 521 odd socks, 75 barbies and 28 games controllers. After moving from the US to a much smaller house in the UK the family no longer have the space they need. Carpenter Rob creates some floor to ceiling storage for the family while Dilly reorganises the kitchen and laundry area

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before it's too late. The trio help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels, let go of the past and move forward

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan insists that the house is fitted with an expensive, over-the-top burglar alarm after Brigitte thinks her van has been stolen amid fears of the rising crime rate. However, it fails to serve its purpose. Brigitte hopes to improve the surgery's atmosphere with some New Age crystals

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers' thoughts turn to death. Michael worries about life expectancy and Ben wonders why God has not made the process of dying as enjoyable as sex. Meanwhile, Susan is shaken by bad news about a lost tourist

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben watches in disbelief as Susan clears his wardrobe, ditching the old clothes that remind him of his free and easy past. To make matters worse, his eldest son breaks his favourite chair, while 12-year-old Michael prepares for his first date - forcing dad into giving him a bit of advice

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben discovers Nick is taking Viagra, but worse is to come when Susan suggests he should try it as well - an idea that ends in a trip to casualty and further embarrassment at yoga classes. Domestic comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben finds out that all Janey's suitors are part-time teachers and Susan discovers that employing Nick is not for the faint-hearted. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker, Daniela Denby-Ashe and Kris Marshall

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan hold their breath as they wait to see if they are expecting a baby, fearful they could produce another one just like Nick. Ben's new assistant Sally puts all his patients off by reacting queasily to the blood that is part and parcel of the job

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 'puppy special' recalling the amazing stories of some of the youngest, fluffiest animals for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began. They include three-month-old springer spaniel Poppy, who was treated in 2016 when she presented with some of the worst front leg deformities Noel had ever seen. In 2018, nine-month-old Jack Russell Bella was admitted as an emergency after being injured in a freak accident, falling from a carrier on a bike and badly fracturing her pelvis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A rescue special, featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the animals for whom Noel has provided extraordinary and life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories. Animals featured include rescue whippet Wobble, who at only a year old in 2015 was wobbling due to a compression fracture in her neck and German shepherd rescue Bran, who first came to Fitzpatricks in 2019 and received an innovative custom total hip replacement - the first of its kind for a three-legged dog

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Bedlington terrier, who has suffered a broken front leg three times in the last four years, is referred to Noel with another fracture in her leg. Elsewhere, beloved fox red Labrador Jethro has arthritic elbows, which have left him unable to walk properly and are causing him great pain. Noel uses a tiny camera to take a closer look at the joint damage and determine the best course of action

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews treat a boy hit by a car outside school, a pensioner with a huge lump on his head and a woman with suspected sepsis

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The medics help a man bleeding profusely, another who is in agony after falling and a woman with a cripplingly painful bowel condition

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Dr Fox is in the visitors' room for a change when his fiancee Sally has an operation to remove her wisdom teeth. Another patient has surgery to have a double hernia repaired

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: One patient is flown home from holiday for an urgent operation to repair a detached retina, while another has a leaky vein that has caused an ulcer in his leg

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. programme.desc: Hundreds of film industry professionals are preyed upon by the Hollywood Con Queen, who defrauded them of over $1.5 million in a campaign lasting several years

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 'puppy special' recalling the amazing stories of some of the youngest, fluffiest animals for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began. They include three-month-old springer spaniel Poppy, who was treated in 2016 when she presented with some of the worst front leg deformities Noel had ever seen. In 2018, nine-month-old Jack Russell Bella was admitted as an emergency after being injured in a freak accident, falling from a carrier on a bike and badly fracturing her pelvis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A rescue special, featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the animals for whom Noel has provided extraordinary and life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories. Animals featured include rescue whippet Wobble, who at only a year old in 2015 was wobbling due to a compression fracture in her neck and German shepherd rescue Bran, who first came to Fitzpatricks in 2019 and received an innovative custom total hip replacement - the first of its kind for a three-legged dog

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Bedlington terrier, who has suffered a broken front leg three times in the last four years, is referred to Noel with another fracture in her leg. Elsewhere, beloved fox red Labrador Jethro has arthritic elbows, which have left him unable to walk properly and are causing him great pain. Noel uses a tiny camera to take a closer look at the joint damage and determine the best course of action

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews treat a boy hit by a car outside school, a pensioner with a huge lump on his head and a woman with suspected sepsis

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The medics help a man bleeding profusely, another who is in agony after falling and a woman with a cripplingly painful bowel condition

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Dr Fox is in the visitors' room for a change when his fiancee Sally has an operation to remove her wisdom teeth. Another patient has surgery to have a double hernia repaired

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: One patient is flown home from holiday for an urgent operation to repair a detached retina, while another has a leaky vein that has caused an ulcer in his leg

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: First edition of the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedian as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, including a woman frightened of her own shadow, the wife of a cannibal on death row, a couple with a unique approach to parenting, an Irish nurse who will not let patients come between her and her twin passions, and a teenager who would rather be right than happy. From February 2004

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy sketch series featuring the versatile actress. Bernie the Irish nurse is feeling a little romantic

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Nan Taylor, Bernie the incompetent nurse, Margaret the jumpy woman and Essex couple Paul and Sam

programme.title: Death at the Mansion: Rebecca Zahau
programme.desc: The experts conclude their theories on Rebecca's death. A civil trial verdict prompts the San Diego Sheriff's Department to make a long-awaited announcement

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: A former teacher with a dream to set up an educational app is tricked into a business partnership with a wealthy neighbour that will see him lose almost everything

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting and the Death Cleaners help with her fear of discussing 'the end'

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics help a man suffering from anxiety who discovered he might have been exposed to HIV. Crews attend to a man found unconscious in the stairwell of a block of flats

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek and Paul are off to Leeds on a rare father and son road trip to pick up a VW Cabriolet and a Lotus Elite

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy a Scandinavian car and settle on a Saab, as they've never done one before. They would prefer a classic 99, but can only find an older 99 that was bought for a rally but can't go the distance. The hope is that it can somehow be improved

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Adam takes a gamble and leaves the English Channel in the hope of finding Dover sole, one of the most expensive fish in British waters, to help cover his rising financial issues. Up in Scotland, Stephen breaks from the rest of the fleet for some solo fishing but heads towards some stormy weather, leaving him with a decision to make

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus buy a Porsche 923 with a cracked interior, bad paintwork and ripped seats. This driving legend is well past its best, but they still hope to turn a profit

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus lay their hands on a potentially very valuable Volvo. It's great to look at, but doesn't start easily and cannot stop, requiring an overhaul of the ignition and breaks

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus splash out £550 on a garage owner's Mercedes 190. Its previous owner has failed to fix it, but the duo are more confident

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus take on a Mazda MX-5 that's been given some real boy racer attitude, hoping to return it to its former glory

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch discovers fellow inmates at Slade Prison are planning an escape over Christmas - and is so convinced the bid is doomed to fail that he takes refuge in the hospital so he cannot be blamed when the tunnel is found. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1975, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch plans to bring a bit of Christmas spirit to Slade Prison in the form of his own home-made booze. But when Mackay and Barrowclough uncover the stash in his and Godber's cell, the crafty con seems in danger of spending the yuletide period in solitary confinement. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1976, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming takes a tumble at Buckby Locks in Northamptonshire and has a problem with his power supply and battery bank

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A window gets smashed onboard the Naughty Lass and Robbie receives a soaking inside the very wet Blisworth Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: During a cold snap Robbie gets iced in on the Grand Union Canal. Later, his parents pay a visit and help him through Marsworth Locks

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer's epic journey continues on the coast of Madagascar, where he goes spearfishing with the Vezo people, whose lifestyle is under threat from their own rapid population growth. On Mauritius, he visits slums that show a different side to the tourist paradise, and he ends this leg in the Seychelles, meeting an Englishman who is turning his private island into the world's smallest national park. Simon also joins the Dutch Special Forces, training to board pirate ships operating on the high seas

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how, by pushing themselves and their bodies to the limit, mammals have found remarkable ways to survive in the hottest places on earth. In South America, thirsty capuchins need all their natural curiosity as they search for water on the forest floor. Camels roam the vast outback of Australia, where they can go for weeks without water thanks to their distinctive hump as an energy store. White sifaka lemurs hug trees to avoid the heat in Madagascar's spiny forest and the echidna has an even stranger way of keeping cool - it blows snot bubbles

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals why forests are the ultimate test of survival for mammals, including a tiger whose stealth and hunting strategies are put to the test in the heat of an Indian summer. The programme also features footage of young chimpanzees learning how to get honey without angering bees and Siberian flying squirrel gliding elegantly and effortlessly from tree to tree

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team seek out a sturdy, reliable 4x4 to tackle the rugged Yorkshire landscape, but get side-tracked into a slightly rusty Subaru Impreza Turbo 2000

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave Mathewson admires a Punto that's two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A fully restored Class 47 diesel engine is transported from its home at Crewe Heritage Centre in Cheshire to the Ecclesbourne Valley Railway in Derbyshire

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret get stuck for hours in a bank holiday traffic jam, with miles of cars behind them, and nothing but the unwelcome sight of a horse's backside in front. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: Beware the Trickster on the Roof
programme.desc: The Meldrew household falls foul of an evil curse, so Victor resolves to remove it. However, it soon becomes clear the task is easier said than done. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A beautiful looking sports car has been fooling the punters and intriguing Derek. It's a Challenger E-Type - a fibreglass replica created from an E-Type mould

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul and Jack head to Staffordshire to pick up five vehicles from collector Rob's colossal barn, including a Mini and a Morris Minor

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave's in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Augusta to Darwin: The Ghan
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores New South Wales by train, travelling 570 miles from Sydney to the mining city of Broken Hill. He explores the origins of Sydney as a penal colony, visiting the Hyde Park barracks where many of the first convicts were housed. He also tours the city's iconic opera house, and volunteers for the lifesaving club at Bondi Beach. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about people's relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Adelaide to Perth: The Indian Pacific
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure aboard the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway, from Adelaide to Perth. Along the way he hears the story of an enterprising 19th-century Briton with a murky past who made his money growing vines, strikes it lucky in one of the most lucrative goldfields in the world and pays a flying visit to the doctor to hear how patients in remote Western Australia are cared for. He also hears how Edward VIII was caught up in a train accident near Bridgetown and learns about a child migration programme that cast a dark shadow on the history of both the UK and Australia

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming takes a tumble at Buckby Locks in Northamptonshire and has a problem with his power supply and battery bank

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A window gets smashed onboard the Naughty Lass and Robbie receives a soaking inside the very wet Blisworth Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: During a cold snap Robbie gets iced in on the Grand Union Canal. Later, his parents pay a visit and help him through Marsworth Locks

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary exploring the country in the aftermath of the recent humanitarian crisis in which thousands of Rohingya Muslims were driven from their homes by the military. Simon visits Burma's Buddhist heartlands, including the ancient capital of Bagan, and meets the city's controversial nationalist monks. While the region affected by the military crackdown is off limits, he is able to visit the world's biggest refugee camp in Bangladesh, and meet people traumatised by the violence

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon ventures into Burma's highlands, where he attends a fire balloon festival and visits an elephant sanctuary, where the animals can enjoy a peaceful life after working in the logging industry. He also makes a secret journey into one of the country's conflict zones to meet members of a rebel army fighting against the Burmese military

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the country as 50 years of civil war draws to a close in the wake of a recently signed peace deal. He meets guerrilla fighters who have pledged to lay down arms, but also discovers a negative consequence of their disarming, as the paramilitary gangs that control the drugs trade grow in power. Simon travels through the country with coca farmers, who discuss the government support they will need in the turbulent times ahead, as poverty and land ownership becoming pressing issues

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team seek out a sturdy, reliable 4x4 to tackle the rugged Yorkshire landscape, but get side-tracked into a slightly rusty Subaru Impreza Turbo 2000

programme.title: Gorilla Revisited with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The eminent broadcaster and naturalist remembers his encounter with mountain gorillas in Rwanda, recalling how the sequences for the programme Life on Earth were filmed and considering the success of efforts to conserve the great apes

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie visits a former Beatle's home in Henley, uncovers horror history in Marlow and struggles to find a mooring in Maidenhead on the River Thames
The incredible story of one of football's most dramatic triumphs - when Arsenal went to Liverpool on the final day of the 1988-89 First Division needing to win to win the league

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews broaden their cultural horizons with a visit to the BBC in the company of a comatose window cleaner they think is Mrs Warboys' cousin. Later, Victor makes a fresh start with a new job - but soon blots his copybook with a characteristic outburst. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.sub-title: Some Day We'll Laugh About This
programme.desc: Terry carries out some home improvements for Bob and Thelma while they are on holiday. However, he finds himself sidetracked by the attentions of their attractive neighbour Sandra. Classic comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes. First shown in 1974

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob visits the hospital after injuring his foot and his misery is compounded when he has a run-in with Terry. Comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob bids his wife farewell and sets off on a boys-only fishing trip with Terry - but guilt soon gets the better of him. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team seek out a sturdy, reliable 4x4 to tackle the rugged Yorkshire landscape, but get side-tracked into a slightly rusty Subaru Impreza Turbo 2000

programme.title: Gorilla Revisited with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The eminent broadcaster and naturalist remembers his encounter with mountain gorillas in Rwanda, recalling how the sequences for the programme Life on Earth were filmed and considering the success of efforts to conserve the great apes

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie visits a former Beatle's home in Henley, uncovers horror history in Marlow and struggles to find a mooring in Maidenhead on the River Thames
The incredible story of one of football's most dramatic triumphs - when Arsenal went to Liverpool on the final day of the 1988-89 First Division needing to win to win the league

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret get stuck for hours in a bank holiday traffic jam, with miles of cars behind them, and nothing but the unwelcome sight of a horse's backside in front. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: Beware the Trickster on the Roof
programme.desc: The Meldrew household falls foul of an evil curse, so Victor resolves to remove it. However, it soon becomes clear the task is easier said than done. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy a classic Rover, but have to go for a Honda-based 800 coupe. At only £650 it seems too good to be true - and when they turn up, they aren't completely sure whether to proceed

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.sub-title: Some Day We'll Laugh About This
programme.desc: Terry carries out some home improvements for Bob and Thelma while they are on holiday. However, he finds himself sidetracked by the attentions of their attractive neighbour Sandra. Classic comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes. First shown in 1974

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob visits the hospital after injuring his foot and his misery is compounded when he has a run-in with Terry. Comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob bids his wife farewell and sets off on a boys-only fishing trip with Terry - but guilt soon gets the better of him. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Back on the Parsifal III in Sardinia, with his heads of departments returning for another season, Captain Glenn is poised for his smoothest sail yet

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the rescue of more struggling hotels, beginning with a visit to Epstein House in Liverpool where owner Patrick Duggan is having problems keeping his business afloat. The property was the home of the Beatles' manager Brian Epstein and Patrick was sure the guest house would be highly profitable when he bought it, but such expectations have yet to be realised

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build the world's next generation of engineering wonders - structures and machines that are more ambitious and more advanced than anything seen before

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as he discusses with Tommy Martin the high esteem he was held in for club and country while facing battles on and off the pitch

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines an incident in 2003 where two girls were left fighting for their lives, having been caught in an exchange of gunfire between two rival gangs. The case had far-reaching implications, putting gun violence in Britain's inner cities at the top of the political agenda and changing how the courts protect key witnesses

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam's much overdue date doesn't go as planned, and they end up stranded on an island; Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bnell stars

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sam's double date with hospital administrator Grant and his girlfriend Monica, doesn't go quite as planned. Martha organizes a Grease themed movie night in the park. Stars Catherine Bell and James Denton. The Party The Party Cassie and Sam's much overdue date doesn't go as planned and they end up stranded on an island. Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bell and James Denton star

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade ventures into Brazil's cattle country as he searches for a deadly electric eel that has killed three cowboys. To catch his elusive prey he has to combat all his fears, and after a long chase he finally corners it in an unexpected place

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets an energetic Staffie cross who needs help calming down in kennels, falls in love with a rare West African dog called an Azawakh, and coaxes an overbred bulldog to come out of its shell

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets more dogs struggling to find a home, including a golden retriever with an incontinence problem and a boisterous border collie with a bad hip - who needs Paul's help in the hydrotherapy centre. He also calls in on a tiny pregnant mongrel, and spends time with an OAP Patterdale who just needs a bit of company in her twilight years

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: The seven remaining players take on the next Main Match, Safari Race, a game in which contestants must back the winning animals to gain points but they soon start to realise the importance of playing a social game as well as a strategic one

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.desc: Flagg and Altman go head-to-head as they compete to sell their listings in Trousdale, and Tracy gets a sneak peek at a new Bel Air property.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Captain Glenn is hopeful that Colin's plan to rebuild the engine will work, but the guests tell him this is the worst trip ever and specifically blame Daisy's lack of service

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: The 10th series reunion comes to a close as Raquel faces her former friends and the group revisits the girls' trip to Las Vegas and Lake Havasu

programme.title: Dubai Hustle
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of young Brits making it in the world of luxury high-end property in Dubai

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: Following some of European football's top officials as they adapt to the introduction of VAR, giving their frank assessments of some of the most-talked-about decisions

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Best friends Neil and John took over the Vidella Hotel in Blackpool a decade ago, but their love of practical jokes is beginning to wear thin and falling trade has started to take its toll on them. Alex Polizzi tries to turn their struggling business around, but has her work cut out to prove to the owners that a failing hotel is not a laughing matter

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build the world's next generation of engineering wonders - structures and machines that are more ambitious and more advanced than anything seen before

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital has its hands full when a typhoid epidemic breaks out in the community. Things get worse as the disease's source is traced back to the wards and staff struggle to locate it. Dr Weatherill is forced to reprimand Dr Ormerod after catching him sleeping on the job - but also has startling news for him. Drama, starring Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Frankie accidentally injures a man attempting to steal his motorbike, but is stricken with guilt when the thief dies after crashing the vehicle. A woman being treated for severe burns disappears home, where Drs Ormerod and Weatherill discover her family secret, while Alun becomes obsessed by an expensive stereo system. With Scott Taylor, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Robert Rinder examines the case of Claire Throssell. After years in a controlling marriage, she managed to leave, taking her two young sons with her. Life would have been perfect, if it wasn't for Claire's ex-husband Darren Sykes still having access to their children. During one fatal unsupervised visit, Claire's life would be destroyed

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie, Stephanie, and Abigail help Martha throw Claire a baby shower that sparks memories of Cassie's own. Catherine Bell and James Denton stars

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Sam is excited to perform surgery on a star basketball player. Joy makes a discovery and decides to revive the Middleton Exchange tradition. Catherine Bell and James Denton star

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade discovers one of the most extraordinary monsters he has ever encountered when he travels to Japan to explore the truth behind a legend

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul falls in love with a four-month-old shi-tzu with a serious health condition, helps a nervous pair of chihuahuas come out of their shell, and struggles to contain a boisterous mutt who doesn't know her own strength

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a gentle giant Great Dane that is looking for a loving home, and a pair of bickering Lakeland Terriers who need to be taught how to reign in their mischievous behaviour. Elsewhere, he meets a feisty little Dachshund whose enormous bark makes up for his compact stature

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: With £32,000 in the prize pot the players face their sixth Main Match, Diamond Dilemma, a game in which the contestants are randomly split into two teams and need to work together to win as a team but they need to put themselves first to stay safe as an individual

programme.title: The Beckham Feud: Truth and Lies
programme.desc: Documentary examining the explosive rift inside Britain's most famous family, following a public statement from Brooklyn Beckham on Instagram. Journalists who have tracked the family for decades investigate the ways in which fame, loyalty and public scrutiny have strained a once carefully managed family brand

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: A Secrets Revealed episode providing a deeper glimpse into the fallout of the group post-Scandoval

programme.title: Dubai Hustle
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of young brokers making it in the world of luxury high-end property in Dubai

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Julia Roberts talks about her latest role in psychological campus drama After the Hunt. Colin Farrell discusses playing a con man and gambling addict in atmospheric thriller Ballad of a Small Player. Singer Gloria Estefan introduces her new Spanish-language album Raices. And Benedict Cumberbatch talks about playing a newly widowed father in The Thing with Feathers. Plus, Robbie Williams performs Pretty Face from the forthcoming album BRITPOP

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi comes to the aid of John and Ilona, who bought the 10-bedroom Yeoldon House Hotel in Bideford, Devon as a retirement project. Despite having no industry experience, they ploughed their savings into the project. However, the reality has been far from the good life, with out-of-season occupancy below 10 per cent and the pair unable to afford a salary

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Comedian Vic Reeves and his wife Nancy Sorrell put on a dinner party as they play host to four strangers, who have to try and work out their identities by solving cryptic clues. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Ireland's World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner make four mouth-watering dishes with only five main ingredients, starting with a garlic and rosemary focaccia, followed by John's pink spaghetti. Following that are chicken and peanut butter scissor-cut noodles and moreish and fuss-free banana doughnuts.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follows the stories of two women looking for siblings given up for adoption, but for very different reasons. Jeanette Woodyatt wants to find the brother she has never met and who was turned away from the family by her grandmother, while Karen Turnbull yearns to let her twin siblings know that their mother gave them up for adoption so they would have a better life

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Cambridge for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Chester for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Phil Tufnell, Colin Jackson and Lisa Riley answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Samia Longchambon, Amy Dowden and Will Best all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the fourth live semi-final as more acts that made it through the auditions perform in front of the judges and a live audience for a chance at a place in the final. Viewers hold the power to vote for their favourite acts of the night to send through to the ultimate stage of the competition, and one judge has the Golden Buzzer that can reward one outstanding performer with a ticket straight through

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Contestants get the chance to win the biggest cash prize in British TV history with a limitless jackpot. First up are Joe and sister-in-law Jo, who have been prepping by going round the supermarket sizing things up with a tape measure, followed by Steve and son-in-law Jordan, who hope their approach will help propel them into the truly life-changing sums of money on the ladder

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Irish Road Trip
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp head to snowy Northern Ireland, diving into Selkie myths, vampire legends, the origins of hurling, haunted hotels and Derry's epic Halloween festival

programme.title: Protection
programme.desc: Police officer DI Liz Nyles works in the secretive and high-stakes world of Witness Protection. One morning, a devastating incident at work raises grave questions about the integrity of Liz's unit, which is rocked with accusations of corruption. Drama, starring Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Celebrity Health Stories
programme.desc: After sharing her excruciating painful period experiences with her 3.2 million Instagram followers, actress Jacqueline Jossa is on a personal journey to investigate the condition of painful periods with medical experts, and start a fresh conversation about periods

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: DJ Mike Read puts on a dinner party at his Oxfordshire home for four dinner guests as he plays mystery celebrity host. Will they need to take host Brian McFadden's Poptastic bonus to guess Read's identity?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager opens up about life as a manager, taking the Ireland job twice, the 2002 World Cup, and the infamous relationship with Roy Keane

programme.title: Live Women's Six Nations
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 2.30pm). Coverage of both teams' fifth and final match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium. Scotland prevailed 26-19 in Edinburgh when the teams met in last year's competition, but Ireland prevailed 15-12 at Kingspan Stadium in 2024 and will hope to make home advantage count once again this afternoon

programme.title: Bradley & Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes reach northern Thailand, where Barney arranges for him and Bradley to see the jungle on a rollercoaster. Then they visit a market to try the infamous durian - known as the smelliest fruit in the world - before their journey takes them into the hills where they race on wooden go-karts. The next day sees the pair face their biggest challenge - climbing into an ominous cavern named the Anxiety State Crisis Cave - before ending their trip in a temple where they reminisce about their adventures

programme.title: The Great Escapers
programme.desc: Loose Women stars Katie Piper, Denise Welch and Sunetra Sarker head to Spain's Costa Brava, where they compete against one another to deliver the best holiday activities. With the construction of a human tower, a couple of surprises from Denise, an aerial yoga lesson that isn't a zen-like experience for everyone and a forage for flowers in store, which of the Loose Ladies will win the title of Greatest Escaper when the three of them meet to decide the winner?
